entry.content:
Note on Donations:
Because of the nature of our umbrella non-profit, Manna Ministries, all monies donated through Manna will be used only in Slovakia. For us this is not problem: we are planning to do work in Slovakia at some time in the future (see "Slovakia" below). Current work opportunities are keeping us in the United States until the end of the year, however. Please understand this if donating through Manna.

Update on Miriam and Daniel:
Speaking of current work opportunities, a chance to do contracting has come up for Daniel. This is a perfect chance to be freed to continue to work on danielsjourney as well as put some food on the table! This also means your donation can be used directly for the art itself, as opposed to said food! This is a really great thing!

Slovakia:
Part of our vision is to bring a spirit of optimism to this country beset in pessimism. During our visits there over the past three years, we have been almost overwhelmingly inspired by the beauty and culture we find. We wish to contribute to the spirit of the people through the art inspired by the land as well as create a bridge between peoples of the USA and Slovakia. Our primary means for doing this is through photography and writing, and an integration of the two that suggests meaning through unique art experience. See europe01: beauty/decay/order/chaos for an example.

We plan an extended trip to Slovakia to further expand on these themes as soon as possible.

New Work:
A new installation, "hate mail," will be up on the internet shortly. This work is also going to be shown live in various locations. To help with showing this work, continue reading past "Update on Donations," below.

The work deals with many themes present in our lives as of this moment: abuse, hatred, terrorism, meaning, and linear vs. non-linear processing. It promises to be the most profound work thusfar from danielsjourney dot com.

Update on Donations:
Many of you have responded to our support letter favorably. We want you to know that our spirit behind the letter was more to keep you in touch with what we are doing and where we are going as much as it was a call for support. Any type of support is great, included just an email to say "Hi!"!

Also, in light of the events of Sept 11, any charitible monies you have available would be better invested in relief efforts for those who have lost loved ones in the tragedy.

One way you can be of support is by providing show space for danielsjourney. A new installation entitled "hate mail" will be available from the internet, however a digital gallery is really the place for such an installation. The work deals with hate and abuse, including the tragedies of last week. If you can be of assistence in the showing of this work, please contact danielsjourney through the email address available on the website. {dealingwith@yahoo.com}

check all our weblogs for the most up to date info. most pertinent info right here.

but see also: dealingwith
but see also: hopeagainsthope
but see also: hatemail
but see also: nonlinear

go back to danielsjourney dot com
I've posted a new tune on the net, but not yet under our mp3.com page. Here's the link: http://www.geocities.com/dealingwith/you_say.htm

Announcement of digital installation showing:
Hate Mail is going to be shown at Dada in Delray Beach November 15-17. Check danielsjourney for details.

Europe Trip:
Hopefully you won't think poor of us for going to Europe (ahem). We will be in London for two days and Slovakia for two weeks starting this Friday, October 19. Miriam had a trip planned for September 18, but we did not feel ok with going ahead with that trip so close to September 11. So now we are going together, so that even in the worst case (and no we're not afraid of anything) we'll "go" together. The reason for a fall trip was originally to scope out work and living situations for ourselves. That is still a reason, if now a less pressing one; however the main purpose for this trip is to visit Miriam's ailing grandfather. Of course getting some beautiful photography in would be a great plus. So think of us packing our cold weather gear!

Hate Mail:
Hate Mail came off with relatively few hitches, as these things go. No need to belabor the details here when they are all on the site already. Try the "installations" link on the home page to see hate mail, and after your done with that, check out the hate mail blog at hatemail.blogspot.com.

Publicity!
Hate Mail has been featured, along with links to some of my other work, on Greenbelt.org.uk!

Update on Direction:
As our art makes more and more of a social commentary and our hearts turn in the direction of social action, we are looking for avenues that allow us to continue our art in this way. That is, specifically, to bring about broader change through the seeking of story and beauty and the formation of meaning through the same. Even more specifically, to bring optimism to pessimistic circumstances, hope to seemingly hopeless circumstances, and a new economy to those for whom this active one fails time and again. And to put a final point on the nail, our desire is to see Slovakia, Miriam's homeland, rejuvinated spiritually in these manners as it continues to struggle against the western economy and the adjusting of its value system.

We also desire to be able to facilitate others' similar pursuits and be able to equip them in whatever manner they require such.

"I am an evangelist for the arts." -daniel

New Content:
Hellow all:

I've worked long and hard at all this content and design so hopefully you likey some of itey:

Europe01 journal and pictures are finally online (BIG page)

No Christmas letter or pictures from us this year, but please enjoy my contribs to this Christmas season:
'Have a java java christmas'
a little song, '01xmas' (mp3 file)

New index/order page for the photos

New page about our art-activism (check back here soon for more updates)

New page of my a little bit of my people portfolio

My formerly-known-as-hope-against-hope-dot-com-site is now here and waaaay redesigned
*note: there's a pop-up ad from the web server, but if you *don't close it you won't get another one, so just leave it behind your main window...this was the lesser of two evils...yahoo geocities had gotten out of control...

And of course constant updates to all my blogs...

Cheers,

Activist?

Please check out the new section about a new organization we're planning, Novocenenie.

Monay:

We've sold two copies of sincerity prayer so far. Framed and the whole bit.

We received our first donation yesterday.

We are really excited about that. All firsts are good.

At the same time, we're bailing on a non-profit status for danielsjourney. It will be a for-profit arm of a two-arm existence for us.

But please still help us out. One donation isn't exactly quit your day job monay.

The non-profit will be Novocenenie. Check it out on the activist page. You can for now give to us and specify use in Novocenenie, however it won't be tax-deductable. Give us about 6 months on that. :)

Cheers,
Daniel

Website:

danielsjourney.com got a facelift today. if yer here uv probly figured that out already.

Grant Update:

We didn't get past the first round with the grant for which our application is on the activist page.

Site Update:

Those of you on my email list have this update message in your junk mail folder:

***** YOU may HAVE already WON one MILLION th of a DOLLAR *****

**************************************
READ THIS EMAIL ALL THE WAY TO THE END
YOU WON'T BE DISAPPOINTED!!!*

*results vary. you may actually be disappointed

Dear Jack,

Unemployed? Unhappy with the "system"? Kept down by the "man"?

Then do what thousands are doing--create your own website so you can cry about it to the millions of web surfers worldwide! (please note that only 2 people will actually come to your website and only 1 will figure out how to get to your content)

For just a few dollars a month I will teach you how to make gazillions (a new monetary unit soon to be approved by the federal reserve to describe the amount stolen by Kenneth Lay and Dick Cheney) using the following PROVEN TECHNIQUES:

1. Get a cheesy but catchy domain name like http://www.danielsjourney.com. Update your front page monthly to express things like your distaste for big coffee brands or your fascination with the union jack and British culture

2. Write for web magazines that also have no money, insuring your gradual decline into poverty. Examples of such zines are:
http://www.sevenmagazine.org/cake.html
http://www.greenbelt.org.uk/insight/article.php?story_id=217§ion=Comment
http://www.next-wave.org/mar02/dfss.htm

3. Write a book for which you have no contract whatsoever. It also helps if there is no audience for this book and if you write in a style that alienates most of your (supposed) target audience. Change the title of this work every month or so:
http://personal.atl.bellsouth.net/mia/m/i/mill6858/hvsh_title.html

4. Do this twice just for completeness. This time only update sporadically, and only amongst the rest of your blog. Also change this work's title for added confusion:
http://personal.atl.bellsouth.net/mia/m/i/mill6858/collection.htm

5. Be a web design professional. This guarantees that you will have no work. Without work or money you will have no value to society and from a meanings-perspective cease to exist. This way you can continue to update your site ferociously:
http://personal.atl.bellsouth.net/mia/m/i/mill6858/design2.html

6. Take a ton of great photographs and spend godawful amounts of time scanning them, creating crops, laying them out on a web page, and creating an order form for them. This will assure you of 2 sales a year. This will pay for .01 of your photographic expenses and .00001 of your time. This is still better than most dot-coms!!
http://personal.atl.bellsouth.net/mia/m/i/mill6858/photos.html

7. Use convoluted navigation on your website, and use frames so that all the pages that you link to from your email updates will have no navigation on them. Also do the right thing by putting all your update recipients in the BCC header of the email. This way none of your recipients can steal all the addresses and spam them, but at the same time your update will end up in their junk mail folder never to be seen by human eyes again.

I will teach you all this AND MORE for only $79.99!! This is just 12 montly payments of 6.6658333333333333333333333333333!! Apply now!!

"What a baa-gin!" -Eddie Murphy*

*Eddie Murphy was not actually referring to danielsjourney and does not endorse danielsjourney in any way. Actually, we're quite sure that Eddie Murphy has never visited danielsjourney. He does make lovely movies with talking animals, however.

Today:

Is Miriam and my 4th wedding anniversary! Yeah!
Site Update today

News

Things are currently rough in the Miller household....meaning the world is out to get us, not that there are internal probs :)

Finding work is quite hard and despite that fact that we sold our house the $$ is dwindling quickly. Chances are we will both get McJobs soon and hopefully have some time for this thing on the side.

We're sure there are some rich peps out there in the world who could support djdc for even a month longer, but whether they'd be interested in our postmodern, anticonsumerist, antiestablishment art is another matter. But in the meantime if you have a dollar to give, feel free to do just that. One measely dollar. That way we know somebody is out there. :)

Also if you have any bones to throw in the work department...I'm a tech consultant and writer and web designer...Miriam is an amazing financial consultant...

Love,

Daniel

Dear All,

It is bittersweet, but more sweet, with fear and joy, but more joy, that I write you and let you know of the immediate future of the Miriam and Daniel Miller family.

Many of you already know that Miriam has been accepted into the Foreign Service. If you didn't already know, well now you do. And to make a long story short, all this year we have been hoping for a start date of November, which seemed like the earliest possible chance to start since the July and September Foreign Service Institute (I think that's what it's called) classes had been filled already. Well, God had different plans and we are moving before the end of this month. Miriam was slotted into the July class at the last minute when someone withdrew. She must report in Wash. DC on July 1.

Needless to say, this adds a level of excitment, urgency, and hecticness (is that a word?) to our lives for the next few months. The school is for 7 weeks, at which point Miriam will be informed of what country will be her first station. There will be additional training in DC specific to that country, and then we will be off for two years to said unknown country. But we are very excited to go anywhere and experience new cultures, peoples, and in short, live an experience-rich life. (NOT that we don't have that already...which leads me to...)

We are very sad to leave our community of friends and co-journeymen and women in south Florida, but we are optimistic that through the web, email, and trips back home, the distance will be made shorter and the time away quick. You are all of course invited to come visit us wherever we end up. I'll just be chillin'...doing what I do now but long distance...so I would love the company and I'll be sure to take you to all the best local places. I'd be more specific but until we know where we'll end up that will have to wait.

As for danielsjourney, it will continue, hopefully without interruption (depending on the telecommunications available), and I will continue to journal on my blog, update with new photos, write, make music......some things may be more or less viable now because of circumstances, but I have had some big plans for the site and the "organization" (not that it is really one yet). This year I plan a total redesign, more content, more "stuff," and I also would like to start a seperate non-profit art foundation that would fund underground artists and help them get their art to a broader audience.

We are downsizing even more now, and in that effort, before I start listing stuff on ebay (still unsure how we're going to swing all this in two weeks, but we'll figure it out), I wanted to give everyone a shot at our stuff we're getting rid of. Some of it isn't worth anything, and you can have it, other stuff IS worth something, but just make us an offer. We're not too worried about the $ if it is going to someone who needs it or could use it.

Here's the stuff:

  • the Toyota Tercel -- '93, 108,000 miles, great engine, was going to drive this thing forever but alas...oh and really new AC
  • TV Stand -- definately free
  • set of three little tables -- hard to describe but useful...pine...like really simple end tables but smaller
  • Armoire (sp?) -- the thing we have the computer in
  • Garage shelves
  • Lexmark color printer -- works great just needs a cartridge
  • Really old computer -- no mouse, keyboard, or monitor, ?? MHz, multiple 2G hard drives (some not installed); was going to make this into a Linux box, but never got around to it
  • Collection of old Winning Bicycle Racing Illustrated magazines
  • Cycling videos -- 2000 Giro d'Italia, '92 and '93 Tours de France, "Stars and Watercarriers," "La Course en Tete" (the famous Merckx one)
  • Track racing bicycle -- Pinnarello, red, sad to see it go (if we end up near a velodrome maybe i'll end up getting another one, but...)
  • HED Disc (bicycle) wheel with extra track hub
  • Training bike -- Peugot, Araya clincher wheels with new Panaracer "messenger" tires, old Shimana Ultegra 12-speed groupo, new cables, brake hoods, bar tape, including a couple stems (really long stem is on there now for me, but i have shorter ones)

  • ...and I have three photos that are framed and ready to go and I'm doing a fire sale...normally $150, now $75....I have:
    "nada"
    "the day the music died"
    and "bratislava"

    "the day..." is 8x10, matted and framed, "nada" and "bratislava" are actually bigger (9x12?) and not matted, just framed...all are signed and numbered...

Just email (or call) us.

Also if we are currently borrowing any of your stuff, don't worry, we're good about that! We will be calling you soon to see about returning it!

Anyway, thanks for everything and I'll chat at you again soon when things have settled down again in DC.

All our best wishes and prayers,

Daniel Miller
www.danielsjourney.com

From last Sept.:

We're about creating meanings but their usually free and of little use these days. We're about beauty but no-one seems really interested in that anymore. We're about stories but we don't tell them in two hours in an air conditioned warehouse with Stadium Seating and "fresh buttered" popcorn so we're not popular enough to garner 9 bucks from every person we want to tell them to. We're about seeking but we don't talk about being saved or how many people gave their heart to jesus tonight or how many bibles we gave out on the street today so we can't ask for a tithe.

seeking, stories, beauty, meaning. Beautiful stories with meaning. Meaningful beauty within a story.

complete integration of life, worship...eye, ear...mind, soul...crying and laughing before the tear hits your upper lip...

never talking about God again but talking about God every minute for the rest of our lives...saying horrendous and beautiful in the same breath...frying up new constellations in the little dipper...creating metal sculptures with lightning rods on top because lightning always seems so beautiful but so far away...

wishing you peace but knowing that might not be the best thing for you right now...saying you're beautiful even if I can't see it right now because I know that I should...making you close your eyes because it's not so much what you see through that hole in the fence as much as it is what that hole feels like as you grop for it...

Hello from Washington!

We have been here for about a week and are getting adjusted to our new life. Stay tuned to our blogs (daniel and miriam) for more...

I'll be at Greenbelt night of August 22-26 hanging with Grace, Vaux, et. al. Back in the States late on the 27th.

We're going to Sarajevo, Bosnia-Herzegovina. Leaving at the end of May 2003.

I'm showing my photography at Common Grounds coffee shop, side room, for the month of September, 2002. map

01. I'm showing my photography at Common Grounds coffee shop, side room, for the month of September, 2002. map

The following are on sale now at CG's. First five are 11x14 hand b/w prints in 16x20 black metal frames (and in case it's not obvious, these are thumbs, click to see full image):



and these four are set in one piece, each is 5x7 framed in black wood and chained together in a 2x2 fashion:




You, dear visitor, can of course snag these at any time, but perhaps the show is some kind of impetus. :) That's a funny word. Impetus.

02. I'm playing at Common Grounds on Sept. 20. ~8:30. Outside. With John Athayde.
